name: Creamy Smoked Salmon and Dill Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup chopped fresh dill, or 1 tablespoon dried dill
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously butter a 9 1/2-inch-diameter deep dish pie plate.
2. Place one phyllo sheet on the work surface. Cover the remaining sheets with plastic wrap, then with a clean damp towel.
3. Brush the first phyllo sheet with butter and fold it in half lengthwise. Brush the folded side with butter.
4. Cut the folded phyllo into halves across the width.
5. Place one phyllo rectangle, buttered side down, in the prepared pie plate, covering the bottom and letting the pastry overhang by 1/2 inch on one edge.
6. Brush the top of the first phyllo with butter.
7. Place a second phyllo rectangle, buttered side up, in the pie plate, covering the bottom and letting it overhang by another section of the edge by 1/2 inch; brush with butter.
8. Repeat this process with the remaining four phyllo sheets, ensuring that the entire edge is covered to form a crust.
9. Fold any overhanging pastry under to form a smooth crust edge along the pie plate's edge.
10. Brush the crust edges with butter (the tart can be prepared up to 4 hours in advance and stored refrigerated).
11. Preheat oven to 300°F.
12. Whisk egg yolks and mustard together in a medium bowl until blended.
13. Beat in eggs, half and half, whipping cream, smoked salmon, chopped green onions, and dill (fresh or dried) into the yolk mixture. Season with salt and pepper as desired.
14. Pour the mixture into the prepared crust.
15. Bake until the center is set, approximately 50 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool slightly on a rack.
16. Garnish with dill sprigs and serve at room temperature or slightly warm.
